brain.intro: Documents + filesystem on the brain — called directly on the client.

fields.name: pathPrefix
fields.doc: Restrict results to documents under this path prefix (e.g. "/private/notes").

methods.name: getRaw
methods.signature: getRaw(path: string): Promise<{ content: string | null; path: string; }>

methods.name: editDoc
methods.signature: editDoc(input: { path: string; oldStr: string; newStr: string; expectedContentHash?: string; }): Promise<BrainDocument>
methods.doc:
Surgical in-place edit, mirroring Claude Code's `Edit`: replace an exact
`oldStr` with `newStr`. `oldStr` must match the document body exactly once,
or the edit refuses (add surrounding context to disambiguate).

Routes to `PATCH /brain/doc`, which performs the match + replace
server-side inside the write transaction — atomic and uniqueness-checked,
so there is no racy client read-modify-write and metadata is preserved.
Pass `expectedContentHash` for optimistic-concurrency on hot docs.

params.name: input
params.type: { path: string; oldStr: string; newStr: string; expectedContentHash?: string; }

methods.name: context
methods.doc:
One-call recall — fetch the most relevant memory for a natural-language
query and get back a prompt-ready `contextMd` block.

The brain does NO answer generation. Pass `contextMd` verbatim into your
system prompt or user turn; the caller's LLM composes the answer.

Scope: `brain:read`.

fields.name: query
fields.doc: Natural-language question to recall context for.

fields.name: mode
fields.doc: Retrieval depth: auto (default) = the server decides; deep = multi-hop graph expansion; standard = single-pass vector.

fields.name: k
fields.doc: Max semantic hits to return (1–50, default 10).

fields.name: maxEntities
fields.doc: Max entity summaries to include (0–10, default 3).

methods.name: ingest
methods.doc:
Stream conversations or documents into the brain's memory pipeline.

Conversations are routed through the signal-extraction pipeline and produce
entities + facts. Documents are written as extractable notes.

Scope: `brain:write`.

methods.name: writeDocs
methods.doc:
Batch write multiple documents in a single call.
Equivalent to calling `write()` on each document but with one round-trip.

Scope: `brain:write`.

methods.name: patchDocMeta
methods.doc:
Patch metadata (title, tldr, tags) on an existing document without
touching its body. Accepts both the full edit-doc form (oldStr/newStr)
and a metadata-only form — pass `title`, `tldr`, or `tags` to update
metadata only.

The oldStr/newStr form is unchanged: use `editDoc()` for body edits.
Use this overload when you only need to rename, re-summarize, or re-tag.

Scope: `brain:write`.

methods.name: share
methods.signature: share(kind: ShareKind, id: string): Promise<{ shared: boolean; }>

methods.name: withActor
methods.doc:
Return a derived client that sends `X-Unison-Actor: <externalId>` on every
request. The key must carry the `brain:act-as` scope. Shadow users are
auto-created server-side; `/private` scoping isolates actors automatically.

Pass `null` or `undefined` to clear the actor (return a client without
the header).

Throws synchronously if `externalId` is non-null and fails the format check.

methods.name: resolve
methods.signature: resolve(name: string, kindHint?: EntityKind): Promise<BrainEntity | null>

methods.name: list
methods.signature: list(opts?: { limit?: number; includeInvalidated?: boolean; }): Promise<BrainFact[]>

methods.name: create
methods.signature: create(fromId: string, toId: string, kind: string): Promise<void>

methods.name: resolve
methods.signature: resolve(conflictId: string, verdict: MatchVerdict): Promise<{ ok: true; }>

work.intro: Work primitives — folders, documents, tables, records, views, assets, and the CRM/Tasks tables.

methods.name: apply
methods.doc:
Atomically apply Work primitive operations. Set `dryRun` to validate
without writing (routes to `/v1/work/apply:dry-run`).

methods.name: applyDryRun
methods.doc:
Validate operations WITHOUT writing — the dedicated, discoverable dry-run
entry point. Same shape + result as `apply`; routes to
`/v1/work/apply:dry-run`. Prefer this over `apply({ dryRun: true })`.

methods.name: query
methods.doc: Query a Work view by id (filters / sorts come from `query`).

methods.name: records
methods.doc:
List the records of a table directly — no view needed. Pass `tableId` for any
table, or `semanticKind` (company/person/deal/task) to read the canonical
CRM/Tasks table without first discovering its id. Use this to list/count/
summarize the CRM (it is tenant-scoped and never appears in `tree()`).

fields.name: semanticKind
fields.doc: Or resolve the canonical CRM/Tasks table: company | person | deal | task.

methods.name: search
methods.doc: Search folders, artifacts, documents, tables, records, and assets.

methods.name: inspect
methods.doc: Inspect a single primitive by kind + id.

methods.name: tree
methods.doc: Read the folder + artifact tree (optionally scoped to a team space).

fields.name: sizeBytes
fields.doc: Defaults to the byte length of `data`.

methods.name: thread
methods.signature: thread(id: string, opts?: { allowImages?: boolean; }): Promise<JsonRecord>

methods.name: draft
methods.doc:
Draft an email into the in-app review surface (`app:emails`) for the user to
review, edit, approve/send, or discard — the human-in-the-loop email flow.
Use this for ALL drafting (new emails AND replies via `replyToThreadId`).
Drafting needs no Gmail connection; sending (from the surface) does. This is
the only draft path — there is no "save to Gmail Drafts" tool.

fields.name: to
fields.doc:
Recipients for a NEW email. Omit for a reply — when `replyToThreadId` is
set the recipients + subject are derived from the thread automatically.

fields.name: replyToThreadId
fields.doc:
Reply INTO an existing Gmail thread. The server builds the thread headers
(In-Reply-To / References) + recipient sets from the thread, so the draft
sends as a proper threaded reply when the user approves it.

fields.name: replyMode
fields.doc: "reply" = sender only, "reply_all" = everyone on the thread (default for replies).

fields.name: sessionId
fields.doc:
Session the draft attaches to (drives the canvas `app:emails` review
surface). In the agent's Deno sandbox it defaults to the injected
UNISON_SESSION_ID, so the agent normally doesn't pass it.

chat.intro: Workspace chat — channels, messages, DMs, members.

methods.name: search
methods.signature: search(query: string, opts?: { channelId?: string; limit?: number; }): Promise<JsonRecord>

methods.name: threadReplies
methods.signature: threadReplies(threadRootId: string, opts?: { limit?: number; cursor?: string; }): Promise<JsonRecord>

methods.name: openDm
methods.doc: Open or create a 1:1 DM channel with another user; returns `{ channelId }`.

methods.name: members
methods.doc: List/search workspace members (resolve a teammate name to a user id).

methods.name: events
methods.signature: events(opts: { from: string; to: string; calendarIds?: string[]; }): Promise<JsonRecord>

params.name: opts
params.type: { from: string; to: string; calendarIds?: string[]; }

fields.name: requestId
fields.doc: Idempotency key (uuid) — required by the API.

methods.name: search
methods.doc: Search people (CRM records of the "people" object).

methods.name: list
methods.doc:
List people without a query — the CRUD-style entry point agents expect for
symmetry with `work.records`. Delegates to `search` with an empty query
(the people domain is search-backed; there is no separate list endpoint).

methods.name: search
methods.doc:
Web search via the server's web-search proxy. This is the only route to the
open web — the search runs server-side. Use it to verify a claim or gather
external facts before writing.
